1	[project]
2	name = "theseus-researcher-api"
3	version = "0.1.0"
4	description = "Rate-limited Researcher API (methodology subset; no firm data)"
5	readme = "README.md"
6	requires-python = ">=3.11"
7	dependencies = [
8	  "fastapi>=0.115.0",
9	  "uvicorn[standard]>=0.32.0",
10	  "pydantic>=2.0",
11	  "numpy>=1.24",
12	]
13	
14	[project.optional-dependencies]
15	dev = ["httpx>=0.27", "pytest>=8.0"]
16	
17	[build-system]
18	requires = ["setuptools>=69"]
19	build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"
20	
21	[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
22	where = ["."]
23	include = ["researcher_api*"]
24

1	"""
2	Theseus Researcher API — FastAPI application (SP07).
3	
4	Capability-only: researchers supply text; no firm or cross-tenant data is read.
5	"""
6	
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	import os
10	import sys
11	import time
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Annotated, Any
14	
15	def _repo_root() -> Path:
16	    """Monorepo root for bundled docs; set THESEUS_REPO_ROOT in container installs."""
17	    env = os.environ.get("THESEUS_REPO_ROOT")
18	    if env:
19	        return Path(env)
20	    return Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
21	
22	
23	_REPO_ROOT = _repo_root()
24	_NOOSPHERE = _REPO_ROOT / "noosphere"
25	if _NOOSPHERE.is_dir():
26	    sys.path.insert(0, str(_NOOSPHERE))
27	
28	from fastapi import Depends, FastAPI, HTTPException, Request, Response, status
29	from fastapi.encoders import jsonable_encoder
30	from fastapi.exceptions import RequestValidationError
31	from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
32	from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse, PlainTextResponse
33	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, model_validator
34	
35	from researcher_api import __version__
36	from researcher_api.audit import append_audit, body_fingerprint
37	from researcher_api.claims_util import synthetic_claim
38	from researcher_api.config import (
39	    allow_coherence_judge,
40	    api_git_sha,
41	    lookup_api_key,
42	    rate_limit_per_hour,
43	)
44	from researcher_api.problem import Problem
45	from researcher_api.rate_limit import check_rate_limit
46	from researcher_api.scoring_hosted import prob_mid_row, score_prediction_rows
47	
48	_API_PREFIX = "/v1"
49	
50	
51	def _problem_response(p: Problem) -> JSONResponse:
52	    return JSONResponse(
53	        status_code=p.status,
54	        content=jsonable_encoder(p.model_dump(exclude_none=True)),
55	        media_type="application/problem+json",
56	    )
57	
58	
59	app = FastAPI(
60	    title="Theseus Researcher API",
61	    version=__version__,
62	    description="Methodology subset for external investigators. See /docs and docs/researcher-api/.",
63	    openapi_tags=[
64	        {"name": "extract", "description": "Claim extraction from plain text"},
65	        {"name": "coherence", "description": "Six-layer coherence on two claim strings"},
66	        {"name": "embed", "description": "Embedding vectors (researcher texts)"},
67	        {"name": "predict-score", "description": "Brier / log-loss / bins on supplied predictions"},
68	        {"name": "replay", "description": "Time-machine replay (stub in v1)"},
69	        {"name": "methods", "description": "Method notes and version metadata"},
70	        {"name": "register", "description": "Researcher signup (planned)"},
71	        {"name": "security", "description": "Coordinated disclosure policy"},
72	    ],
73	)
74	
75	app.add_middleware(
76	    CORSMiddleware,
77	    allow_origins=["*"],
78	    allow_methods=["*"],
79	    allow_headers=["*"],
80	)
81	
82	
83	@app.exception_handler(RequestValidationError)
84	async def validation_handler(request: Request, exc: RequestValidationError) -> JSONResponse:
85	    p = Problem(
86	        title="Validation error",
87	        status=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY,
88	        detail=str(exc.errors()),
89	        instance=str(request.url),
90	    )
91	    return _problem_response(p)
92	
93	
94	@app.exception_handler(HTTPException)
95	async def http_exc_handler(request: Request, exc: HTTPException) -> JSONResponse:
96	    det = exc.detail
97	    title = det if isinstance(det, str) else "HTTP error"
98	    p = Problem(
99	        title=title,
100	        status=int(exc.status_code),
101	        detail=str(det),
102	        instance=str(request.url),
103	    )
104	    return _problem_response(p)
105	
106	
107	class ApiKeyUser(BaseModel):
108	    label: str
109	    sandbox_tenant_id: str
110	    secret: str
111	
112	
113	def require_api_key(x_api_key: str | None) -> ApiKeyUser:
114	    if not x_api_key:
115	        raise HTTPException(status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, "Missing X-API-Key header")
116	    rec = lookup_api_key(x_api_key.strip())
117	    if rec is None:
118	        raise HTTPException(status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, "Invalid API key")
119	    return ApiKeyUser(label=rec.label, sandbox_tenant_id=rec.sandbox_tenant_id, secret=rec.secret)
120	
121	
122	def get_api_user(request: Request) -> ApiKeyUser:
123	    return require_api_key(request.headers.get("X-API-Key"))
124	
125	
126	def _attach_version_headers(response: Response) -> None:
127	    response.headers["X-Theseus-API-Version"] = __version__
128	    response.headers["X-Theseus-Git-SHA"] = api_git_sha()
129	    response.headers["X-Theseus-LLM-Disclaimer"] = "structured-only"
130	
131	
132	async def _gate_rate_limit(request: Request, user: ApiKeyUser) -> None:
133	    route = request.scope.get("path", "")
134	    ok, retry = check_rate_limit(
135	        api_key=user.secret, route=route, limit_per_hour=rate_limit_per_hour()
136	    )
137	    if not ok:
138	        raise HTTPException(
139	            status.HTTP_429_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,
140	            f"Rate limit exceeded; retry after {retry}s",
141	            headers={"Retry-After": str(retry)},
142	        )
143	
144	
145	# ── Request bodies ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
146	
147	
148	class ExtractClaimsBody(BaseModel):
149	    text: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=120_000)
150	
151	
152	class CoherenceBody(BaseModel):
153	    claim_a: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=16_000)
154	    claim_b: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=16_000)
155	    layers: list[str] | None = Field(
156	        default=None,
157	        description="Optional subset hint; judge layer only if server enables RESEARCHER_API_COHERENCE_JUDGE=1",
158	    )
159	
160	
161	class EmbedBody(BaseModel):
162	    texts: list[str] = Field(min_length=1, max_length=64)
163	
164	    @model_validator(mode="after")
165	    def _text_lengths(self) -> EmbedBody:
166	        for t in self.texts:
167	            if len(t) > 8000:
168	                raise ValueError("each text must be <= 8000 characters")
169	        return self
170	
171	
172	class PredictRow(BaseModel):
173	    prob_low: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
174	    prob_high: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
175	    outcome: int = Field(ge=0, le=1)
176	
177	    def mid(self) -> float:
178	        lo, hi = self.prob_low, self.prob_high
179	        if lo > hi:
180	            lo, hi = hi, lo
181	        return prob_mid_row(lo, hi)
182	
183	
184	class PredictScoreBody(BaseModel):
185	    predictions: list[PredictRow] = Field(min_length=1, max_length=10_000)
186	
187	
188	class ReplayBody(BaseModel):
189	    corpus_note: str = Field(default="", max_length=2000)
190	    as_of: str = Field(description="ISO date YYYY-MM-DD", pattern=r"^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$")
191	
192	
193	# ── Lazy embedder ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
194	
195	_embedder: Any = None
196	
197	
198	def _get_embedder() -> Any:
199	    global _embedder
200	    if _embedder is None:
201	        from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
202	
203	        from noosphere.config import get_settings
204	
205	        s = get_settings()
206	        _embedder = SentenceTransformer(s.embedding_model_name, device=s.embedding_device)
207	    return _embedder
208	
209	
210	# ── Routes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
211	
212	
213	@app.get("/healthz")
214	def healthz() -> dict[str, str]:
215	    return {"status": "ok"}
216	
217	
218	@app.get("/security", tags=["security"])
219	def security_policy() -> PlainTextResponse:
220	    """Coordinated disclosure policy (SP08); Markdown served as text/plain."""
221	    path = _REPO_ROOT / "docs" / "researcher-api" / "SECURITY.md"
222	    if not path.is_file():
223	        return PlainTextResponse("Security policy not bundled in this deployment.\n", status_code=404)
224	    return PlainTextResponse(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"), media_type="text/plain; charset=utf-8")
225	
226	
227	@app.post(f"{_API_PREFIX}/extract-claims", tags=["extract"])
228	async def extract_claims(
229	    request: Request,
230	    response: Response,
231	    body: ExtractClaimsBody,
232	    user: Annotated[ApiKeyUser, Depends(get_api_user)],
233	) -> dict[str, Any]:
234	    await _gate_rate_limit(request, user)
235	    t0 = time.perf_counter()
236	    raw = await request.body()
237	    try:
238	        from noosphere.claim_extractor import ClaimExtractor

1	# Theseus Researcher API (SP07)
2	
3	Documented, rate-limited HTTP surface for **outside investigators**: claim extraction, coherence, embeddings, calibration scoring, and (stub) replay — on **researcher-supplied text only**. No access to firm tenants or internal stores.
4	
5	## Run (development)
6	
7	From the **monorepo root** (so `noosphere` is importable):
8	
9	```bash
10	cd researcher_api
11	python3 -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
12	pip install -e ".[dev]"
13	export PYTHONPATH="$(pwd)/../noosphere${PYTHONPATH:+:$PYTHONPATH}"
14	export RESEARCHER_API_KEYS="demo:sandbox-demo:sk-demo-researcher-key-0001"
15	export THESEUS_GIT_SHA="$(git rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo unknown)"
16	uvicorn researcher_api.main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8088
17	```
18	
19	- OpenAPI: http://127.0.0.1:8088/docs  
20	- Alternate Redoc: http://127.0.0.1:8088/redoc  
21	
22	Send `X-API-Key: sk-demo-researcher-key-0001` on every request.
23	
24	## Deploy
25	
26	See `deploy/docker/researcher-api/Dockerfile`. The service is **separate** from the Founder Portal.
27	
28	## Policies & citation
29	
30	- `docs/researcher-api/ACCEPTABLE_USE.md`  
31	- `docs/researcher-api/PRIVACY.md`  
32	- `docs/researcher-api/CITATION.bib` (Zenodo DOI placeholder until minted)

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import os
4	from dataclasses import dataclass
5	from pathlib import Path
6	from functools import lru_cache
7	
8	
9	@dataclass(frozen=True)
10	class ApiKeyRecord:
11	    label: str
12	    sandbox_tenant_id: str
13	    secret: str
14	
15	
16	@lru_cache
17	def _parse_keys() -> dict[str, ApiKeyRecord]:
18	    """
19	    RESEARCHER_API_KEYS format (comma-separated):
20	      label:sandbox_tenant:secret,label2:tenant2:secret2
21	    """
22	    raw = os.environ.get("RESEARCHER_API_KEYS", "").strip()
23	    out: dict[str, ApiKeyRecord] = {}
24	    if not raw:
25	        return out
26	    for part in raw.split(","):
27	        part = part.strip()
28	        if not part:
29	            continue
30	        bits = part.split(":")
31	        if len(bits) < 3:
32	            continue
33	        secret = bits[-1]
34	        tenant = bits[-2]
35	        label = ":".join(bits[:-2])
36	        out[secret] = ApiKeyRecord(label=label, sandbox_tenant_id=tenant, secret=secret)
37	    return out
38	
39	
40	def lookup_api_key(secret: str) -> ApiKeyRecord | None:
41	    return _parse_keys().get(secret)
42	
43	
44	def api_git_sha() -> str:
45	    return os.environ.get("THESEUS_GIT_SHA", "unknown").strip() or "unknown"
46	
47	
48	def audit_log_path() -> str:
49	    raw = os.environ.get(
50	        "THESEUS_RESEARCHER_AUDIT_LOG", "~/.theseus/researcher_api_audit.jsonl"
51	    )
52	    return str(Path(os.path.expandvars(raw)).expanduser())
53	
54	
55	def rate_limit_per_hour() -> int:
56	    return int(os.environ.get("RESEARCHER_API_RATE_LIMIT_PER_HOUR", "120"))
57	
58	
59	def allow_coherence_judge() -> bool:
60	    """Structured judge only; off by default (heavy + abuse surface)."""
61	    return os.environ.get("RESEARCHER_API_COHERENCE_JUDGE", "").lower() in ("1", "true", "yes")

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import hashlib
4	import json
5	import os
6	import threading
7	import time
8	from pathlib import Path
9	from typing import Any
10	
11	_lock = threading.Lock()
12	
13	from researcher_api.config import audit_log_path
14	
15	
16	def append_audit(
17	    *,
18	    api_key_label: str,
19	    sandbox_tenant_id: str,
20	    route: str,
21	    request_sha256: str,
22	    latency_ms: float,
23	    cost_units: float,
24	    ok: bool,
25	    status_code: int,
26	) -> None:
27	    row: dict[str, Any] = {
28	        "ts": time.time(),
29	        "api_key_label": api_key_label,
30	        "sandbox_tenant_id": sandbox_tenant_id,
31	        "route": route,
32	        "request_sha256": request_sha256,
33	        "latency_ms": round(latency_ms, 2),
34	        "cost_units": cost_units,
35	        "ok": ok,
36	        "status_code": status_code,
37	    }
38	    path = Path(audit_log_path()).expanduser()
39	    line = json.dumps(row, default=str) + "\n"
40	    with _lock:
41	        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
42	        with path.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
43	            f.write(line)
44	
45	
46	def body_fingerprint(raw_body: bytes) -> str:
47	    return hashlib.sha256(raw_body).hexdigest()

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import threading
4	import time
5	from collections import defaultdict, deque
6	
7	_lock = threading.Lock()
8	_events: dict[tuple[str, str], deque[float]] = defaultdict(deque)
9	
10	
11	def check_rate_limit(*, api_key: str, route: str, limit_per_hour: int) -> tuple[bool, int]:
12	    """
13	    Sliding window per (api_key, route). Returns (allowed, retry_after_sec).
14	    """
15	    now = time.monotonic()
16	    window = 3600.0
17	    key = (api_key, route)
18	    with _lock:
19	        q = _events[key]
20	        while q and now - q[0] > window:
21	            q.popleft()
22	        if len(q) >= limit_per_hour:
23	            oldest = q[0]
24	            retry_after = int(max(1.0, window - (now - oldest)))
25	            return False, retry_after
26	        q.append(now)
27	    return True, 0

1	"""
2	Conclusions Registry for the Noosphere System.
3	
4	This module provides a separate store for SUBSTANTIVE claims (conclusions about
5	the world) that the firm's founders make. Conclusions are tracked with method
6	attribution so they can feed back into calibration of the Noosphere's
7	methodological brain.
8	
9	The firm's core brain stores only METHODOLOGICAL knowledge — how to think.
10	But substantive conclusions are valuable as calibration data: if the firm
11	predicts "AI will transform healthcare by 2028" using analogical reasoning,
12	and that prediction turns out wrong, that's evidence about the reliability
13	of analogical reasoning in that domain.
14	
15	This creates a closed-loop system where substantive track records improve
16	methodological decision-making over time.
17	"""
18	
19	import json
20	from datetime import date, datetime, timedelta
21	from pathlib import Path
22	from typing import Optional, List, Dict, Tuple
23	from enum import Enum
24	import uuid
25	
26	from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field
27	
28	from noosphere.llm import LLMClient, llm_client_from_settings
29	from noosphere.models import Claim, Principle
30	from noosphere.observability import get_logger
31	
32	logger = get_logger(__name__)
33	
34	
35	# ── Enums ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36	
37	class ReasoningMethod(str, Enum):
38	    """Fundamental reasoning methods used in substantive reasoning."""
39	    DEDUCTION = "deduction"                  # From general principles to specific
40	    INDUCTION = "induction"                  # From specific observations to general
41	    ANALOGY = "analogy"                      # Reasoning by structural similarity
42	    EMPIRICAL = "empirical"                  # Based on direct observation/data
43	    BASE_RATE = "base_rate"                  # Using statistical/historical frequencies
44	    FIRST_PRINCIPLES = "first_principles"    # From axioms and fundamental truths
45	    AUTHORITY = "authority"                  # Citing expert sources
46	    PATTERN_MATCHING = "pattern_matching"    # Recognizing recurring structures
47	    THOUGHT_EXPERIMENT = "thought_experiment" # Hypothetical reasoning
48	    ABDUCTION = "abduction"                  # Inference to best explanation
49	    UNKNOWN = "unknown"                      # Method not classified
50

52	class ResolutionStatus(str, Enum):
53	    """Status of a prediction or falsifiable claim."""
54	    UNRESOLVED = "unresolved"
55	    RESOLVED_CORRECT = "resolved_correct"
56	    RESOLVED_INCORRECT = "resolved_incorrect"
57	    PARTIALLY_CORRECT = "partially_correct"
58	    UNRESOLVABLE = "unresolvable"

61	# ── Core Models ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
62	
63	class SubstantiveConclusion(BaseModel):
64	    """
65	    A substantive claim about the world made by the firm's founders.
66	
67	    Conclusions are atomic propositions that can be true or false, attributed
68	    to a speaker, produced by a specific reasoning method, and (optionally)
69	    falsifiable. The system tracks resolution outcomes to calibrate method
70	    reliability over time.
71	
72	    Attributes:
73	        id: Unique identifier (UUID)
74	        text: The substantive claim itself
75	        speaker_id: ID of the founder who made it
76	        speaker_name: Name of the founder
77	        episode_id: Which episode it appeared in
78	        episode_date: When the episode aired
79	        domain: Topic domain (e.g., "technology", "macro_economics")
80	        method_used: Which reasoning method produced this
81	        confidence_expressed: How confident the speaker seemed (0-1)
82	        is_prediction: Whether this is falsifiable about the future
83	        falsification_condition: What would prove it wrong
84	        resolution_date: When we can check if it's right
85	        resolved: Whether outcome has been determined
86	        resolution_evidence: What evidence resolved it
87	        created_at: When this conclusion was recorded
88	        methodological_context: Surrounding discourse about HOW it was reached
89	        linked_methodology_ids: IDs of methodological principles that produced it
90	    """
91	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
92	    text: str
93	    speaker_id: str
94	    speaker_name: str
95	    episode_id: str
96	    episode_date: date
97	    domain: str  # e.g., "macro_economics", "technology", "investing", "geopolitics", "science"
98	    method_used: ReasoningMethod = ReasoningMethod.UNKNOWN
99	    confidence_expressed: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
100	    is_prediction: bool = False
101	    falsification_condition: Optional[str] = None
102	    resolution_date: Optional[date] = None
103	    resolved: Optional[bool] = None
104	    resolution_evidence: Optional[str] = None
105	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
106	    methodological_context: str = ""  # Surrounding discourse
107	    linked_methodology_ids: List[str] = []  # Principle IDs
108	
109	    class Config:
110	        use_enum_values = True
111	
112	    def status(self) -> ResolutionStatus:
113	        """Determine resolution status."""
114	        if self.resolved is None:
115	            return ResolutionStatus.UNRESOLVED
116	        elif self.resolved is True:
117	            return ResolutionStatus.RESOLVED_CORRECT
118	        else:
119	            return ResolutionStatus.RESOLVED_INCORRECT
120

122	class MethodAccuracyRecord(BaseModel):

125	
126	    Tracks how well a particular reasoning method performs, enabling
127	    the calibration feedback loop.
128	    """
129	    method_name: ReasoningMethod
130	    domain: str
131	    total_conclusions: int = 0
132	    resolved_conclusions: int = 0
133	    correct_count: int = 0
134	    incorrect_count: int = 0
135	    accuracy_rate: Optional[float] = None
136	    brier_score: Optional[float] = None
137	    average_confidence: float = 0.0
138	    calibration_error: Optional[float] = None  # abs(confidence - accuracy)
139	
140	    class Config:
141	        use_enum_values = True

175	# ── ConclusionsRegistry ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
176	
177	class ConclusionsRegistry:
178	    """
179	    Persistent store for substantive conclusions with resolution tracking.
180	
181	    Provides methods to:
182	    - Register new conclusions
183	    - Record resolution outcomes
184	    - Query conclusions by method, domain, or status
185	    - Calculate accuracy metrics for methods
186	    - Generate calibration feedback
187	    """
188	
189	    def __init__(self, data_path: str = "conclusions_registry.json"):
190	        """
191	        Initialize the registry.
192	
193	        Args:
194	            data_path: Path to persistent JSON store
195	        """
196	        self.data_path = Path(data_path)
197	        self.conclusions: Dict[str, SubstantiveConclusion] = {}
198	        self.open_questions: Dict[str, OpenQuestionCandidate] = {}
199	        self.load()
200	
201	    def register_open_question(self, oq: OpenQuestionCandidate) -> str:
202	        """Persist an unresolved-coherence open question for founder review."""
203	        self.open_questions[oq.id] = oq
204	        self.save()
205	        logger.info("Registered open question candidate %s", oq.id)
206	        return oq.id
207	
208	    def register(self, conclusion: SubstantiveConclusion) -> str:
209	        """
210	        Register a new substantive conclusion.
211	
212	        Args:
213	            conclusion: SubstantiveConclusion object to register
214	
215	        Returns:
216	            The conclusion's ID
217	        """
218	        if not conclusion.id:
219	            conclusion.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
220	
221	        self.conclusions[conclusion.id] = conclusion
222	        logger.info(
223	            f"Registered conclusion {conclusion.id} "
224	            f"({conclusion.method_used}:{conclusion.domain}): "
225	            f"{conclusion.text[:60]}..."
226	        )
227	        self.save()
228	        return conclusion.id
229	
230	    def resolve(
231	        self,
232	        conclusion_id: str,
233	        outcome: bool,
234	        evidence: str = ""
235	    ) -> SubstantiveConclusion:
236	        """
237	        Record whether a conclusion turned out to be correct.
238	
239	        Args:
240	            conclusion_id: ID of the conclusion
241	            outcome: True if correct, False if incorrect
242	            evidence: Supporting evidence for the resolution
243	
244	        Returns:
245	            Updated SubstantiveConclusion object
246	
247	        Raises:
248	            ValueError: If conclusion_id not found
249	        """
250	        if conclusion_id not in self.conclusions:
251	            raise ValueError(f"Conclusion {conclusion_id} not found")
252	
253	        conclusion = self.conclusions[conclusion_id]
254	        conclusion.resolved = outcome
255	        conclusion.resolution_evidence = evidence
256	
257	        logger.info(
258	            f"Resolved conclusion {conclusion_id}: "
259	            f"{'CORRECT' if outcome else 'INCORRECT'}"
260	        )
261	        self.save()
262	        return conclusion
263	
264	    def get_by_method(self, method: str) -> List[SubstantiveConclusion]:
265	        """
266	        Get all conclusions produced by a given reasoning method.
267	
268	        Args:
269	            method: ReasoningMethod enum value (as string)
270	
271	        Returns:
272	            List of conclusions using that method
273	        """
274	        return [
275	            c for c in self.conclusions.values()
276	            if str(c.method_used) == method
277	        ]
278	
279	    def get_by_domain(self, domain: str) -> List[SubstantiveConclusion]:
280	        """
281	        Get all conclusions in a specific domain.
282	
283	        Args:
284	            domain: Domain name (e.g., "technology", "economics")
285	
286	        Returns:
287	            List of conclusions in that domain
288	        """
289	        return [c for c in self.conclusions.values() if c.domain == domain]
290	
291	    def get_unresolved(self) -> List[SubstantiveConclusion]:
292	        """
293	        Get all conclusions awaiting resolution.
294	
295	        Returns:
296	            List of unresolved conclusions
297	        """
298	        return [c for c in self.conclusions.values() if c.resolved is None]
299	
300	    def get_predictions_due(
301	        self,
302	        as_of: Optional[date] = None
303	    ) -> List[SubstantiveConclusion]:
304	        """
305	        Get predictions whose resolution date has passed but aren't resolved yet.
306	
307	        Args:
308	            as_of: Date to check against (default: today)
309	
310	        Returns:
311	            List of overdue predictions
312	        """
313	        if as_of is None:
314	            as_of = date.today()
315	
316	        return [
317	            c for c in self.conclusions.values()
318	            if (
319	                c.is_prediction
320	                and c.resolution_date
321	                and c.resolution_date <= as_of
322	                and c.resolved is None
323	            )
324	        ]
325	
326	    def method_accuracy(
327	        self,
328	        method: str,
329	        domain: Optional[str] = None
330	    ) -> MethodAccuracyRecord:
331	        """
332	        Calculate accuracy statistics for a reasoning method.
333	
334	        Args:
335	            method: ReasoningMethod value
336	            domain: Optional domain to filter by
337	
338	        Returns:
339	            MethodAccuracyRecord with accuracy metrics
340	        """
341	        conclusions = self.get_by_method(method)
342	        if domain:
343	            conclusions = [c for c in conclusions if c.domain == domain]
344	
345	        resolved = [c for c in conclusions if c.resolved is not None]
346	        correct = [c for c in resolved if c.resolved is True]
347	        incorrect = [c for c in resolved if c.resolved is False]
348	
349	        accuracy_rate = None
350	        brier_score = None
351	        calibration_error = None
352	
353	        if resolved:
354	            accuracy_rate = len(correct) / len(resolved)
355	
356	            # Brier score: mean squared error of confidence vs outcome
357	            brier_score = sum(
358	                (c.confidence_expressed - (1.0 if c.resolved else 0.0)) ** 2
359	                for c in resolved
360	            ) / len(resolved)
361	
362	            avg_confidence = (
363	                sum(c.confidence_expressed for c in conclusions) / len(conclusions)
364	                if conclusions else 0.0
365	            )
366	            calibration_error = abs(avg_confidence - accuracy_rate)
367	        else:
368	            avg_confidence = (
369	                sum(c.confidence_expressed for c in conclusions) / len(conclusions)
370	                if conclusions else 0.0
371	            )
372	
373	        return MethodAccuracyRecord(
374	            method_name=method,
375	            domain=domain or "all",
376	            total_conclusions=len(conclusions),
377	            resolved_conclusions=len(resolved),
378	            correct_count=len(correct),
379	            incorrect_count=len(incorrect),
380	            accuracy_rate=accuracy_rate,
381	            brier_score=brier_score,
382	            average_confidence=avg_confidence,
383	            calibration_error=calibration_error,
384	        )
385	
386	    def all_method_accuracies(self) -> List[MethodAccuracyRecord]:
387	        """
388	        Calculate accuracy breakdown by method × domain.
389	
390	        Returns:
391	            List of MethodAccuracyRecords for all combinations
392	        """
393	        records = []
394	        methods = set(str(c.method_used) for c in self.conclusions.values())
395	        domains = set(c.domain for c in self.conclusions.values())
396	
397	        # Calculate by method × domain
398	        for method in methods:
399	            for domain in domains:
400	                record = self.method_accuracy(method, domain)
401	                if record.total_conclusions > 0:
402	                    records.append(record)
403	
404	            # Also include overall by method
405	            record = self.method_accuracy(method)
406	            if record.total_conclusions > 0:
407	                records.append(record)
408	
409	        return records
410	
411	    def save(self) -> None:
412	        """Persist conclusions to JSON."""
413	        try:
414	            data = {
415	                "conclusions": [
416	                    json.loads(c.model_dump_json())
417	                    for c in self.conclusions.values()
418	                ],
419	                "open_questions": [
420	                    json.loads(o.model_dump_json())
421	                    for o in self.open_questions.values()
422	                ],
423	            }
424	            # Convert datetime objects to ISO format strings
425	            for item in data["conclusions"]:
426	                item["created_at"] = item["created_at"]
427	                if item.get("resolution_evidence"):
428	                    pass  # Already a string
429	
430	            self.data_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
431	            with open(self.data_path, 'w') as f:
432	                json.dump(data, f, indent=2, default=str)
433	            logger.info(f"Registry saved to {self.data_path}")
434	        except Exception as e:
435	            logger.error(f"Failed to save registry: {e}")
436	            raise
437	
438	    def load(self) -> None:
439	        """Load conclusions from JSON."""
440	        if not self.data_path.exists():
441	            logger.info(f"Registry file {self.data_path} not found, starting fresh")
442	            return
443	
444	        try:
445	            with open(self.data_path, 'r') as f:
446	                data = json.load(f)
447	
448	            self.conclusions = {}
449	            for item in data.get("conclusions", []):
450	                # Convert ISO datetime strings back to datetime
451	                if isinstance(item.get("created_at"), str):
452	                    item["created_at"] = datetime.fromisoformat(item["created_at"])
453	
454	                conclusion = SubstantiveConclusion(**item)
455	                self.conclusions[conclusion.id] = conclusion
456	
457	            self.open_questions = {}
458	            for item in data.get("open_questions", []):
459	                if isinstance(item.get("created_at"), str):
460	                    item["created_at"] = datetime.fromisoformat(item["created_at"])
461	                oq = OpenQuestionCandidate(**item)
462	                self.open_questions[oq.id] = oq
463	
464	            logger.info(
465	                f"Loaded {len(self.conclusions)} conclusions and "
466	                f"{len(self.open_questions)} open questions from {self.data_path}"
467	            )
468	        except Exception as e:
469	            logger.error(f"Failed to load registry: {e}")
470	            raise
471	
472	    def search(self, query: str, k: int = 10) -> List[ConclusionSummary]:
473	        """
474	        Simple text search over conclusions.
475	
476	        Args:
477	            query: Search query string
478	            k: Number of results to return
479	
480	        Returns:
481	            List of matching conclusions (up to k)
482	        """
483	        query_lower = query.lower()
484	        matches = []
485	
486	        for conclusion in self.conclusions.values():
487	            if (
488	                query_lower in conclusion.text.lower()
489	                or query_lower in conclusion.speaker_name.lower()
490	                or query_lower in conclusion.domain.lower()
491	            ):
492	                matches.append(
493	                    ConclusionSummary(
494	                        id=conclusion.id,
495	                        text=conclusion.text,
496	                        speaker_name=conclusion.speaker_name,
497	                        domain=conclusion.domain,
498	                        method_used=str(conclusion.method_used),
499	                        confidence_expressed=conclusion.confidence_expressed,
500	                        is_prediction=conclusion.is_prediction,
501	                        resolved=conclusion.resolved,
502	                        created_at=conclusion.created_at,
503	                    )
504	                )
505	
506	        # Return top k matches
507	        return matches[:k]
508	
509	
510	# ── CalibrationAnalyzer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
511	
512	class CalibrationAnalyzer:
513	    """
514	    Analyzes the firm's reasoning method performance to generate
515	    calibration feedback for the methodological brain.
516	
517	    This is the KEY integration point: it transforms substantive accuracy
518	    data into methodological observations that the main brain CAN store.
519	    """
520	
521	    def __init__(self, registry: ConclusionsRegistry):
522	        """
523	        Initialize the analyzer.
524	
525	        Args:
526	            registry: ConclusionsRegistry to analyze
527	        """
528	        self.registry = registry
529	
530	    def method_reliability_report(self) -> Dict:
531	        """
532	        Generate comprehensive reliability assessment for each reasoning method.
533	
534	        For each method the firm uses, provides:
535	        - Number of conclusions produced
536	        - Accuracy rate (where resolvable)
537	        - Average confidence vs. actual accuracy (calibration)
538	        - Domains where the method works best/worst
539	        - Actionable recommendations
540	
541	        Returns:
542	            Dictionary with reliability data for each method
543	        """
544	        accuracy_records = self.registry.all_method_accuracies()
545	
546	        # Group by method
547	        by_method: Dict[str, List[MethodAccuracyRecord]] = {}
548	        for record in accuracy_records:
549	            if record.method_name not in by_method:
550	                by_method[record.method_name] = []
551	            by_method[record.method_name].append(record)
552	
553	        report = {}
554	        for method, records in by_method.items():
555	            # Find overall record (domain="all")
556	            overall = next(
557	                (r for r in records if r.domain == "all"),
558	                None
559	            )
560	
561	            if not overall:
562	                continue
563	
564	            domain_records = [r for r in records if r.domain != "all"]
565	            domain_records.sort(
566	                key=lambda r: r.accuracy_rate if r.accuracy_rate else 0.0,
567	                reverse=True
568	            )
569	
570	            best_domains = [r.domain for r in domain_records[:3] if r.accuracy_rate]
571	            worst_domains = [r.domain for r in domain_records[-3:] if r.accuracy_rate]
572	
573	            # Determine if well-calibrated
574	            is_calibrated = False
575	            if overall.calibration_error is not None:
576	                # Well-calibrated if confidence-accuracy gap < 0.1
577	                is_calibrated = overall.calibration_error < 0.10
578	
579	            confidence_assessment = "well-calibrated"
580	            if overall.calibration_error and overall.calibration_error > 0.2:
581	                if overall.average_confidence > (overall.accuracy_rate or 0.0):
582	                    confidence_assessment = "overconfident"
583	                else:
584	                    confidence_assessment = "underconfident"
585	
586	            recommendations = self._generate_recommendations(
587	                method, overall, best_domains, worst_domains, is_calibrated
588	            )
589	
590	            report[method] = {
591	                "total_conclusions": overall.total_conclusions,
592	                "resolved_conclusions": overall.resolved_conclusions,
593	                "accuracy_rate": overall.accuracy_rate,
594	                "average_confidence": overall.average_confidence,
595	                "calibration_error": overall.calibration_error,
596	                "brier_score": overall.brier_score,
597	                "confidence_assessment": confidence_assessment,
598	                "best_domains": best_domains,
599	                "worst_domains": worst_domains,
600	                "recommendations": recommendations,
601	            }
602	
603	        return report
604	
605	    def domain_report(self, domain: str) -> Dict:
606	        """
607	        Generate performance analysis for conclusions in a specific domain.
608	
609	        For a given domain, shows:
610	        - Which methods are most/least accurate
611	        - Overall calibration
612	        - Trends over time (if available)
613	
614	        Args:
615	            domain: Domain name to analyze
616	
617	        Returns:
618	            Dictionary with domain analysis
619	        """
620	        conclusions_in_domain = self.registry.get_by_domain(domain)
621	        if not conclusions_in_domain:
622	            return {"error": f"No conclusions found in domain '{domain}'"}
623	
624	        # Group by method
625	        by_method: Dict[str, List[SubstantiveConclusion]] = {}
626	        for c in conclusions_in_domain:
627	            method = str(c.method_used)
628	            if method not in by_method:
629	                by_method[method] = []
630	            by_method[method].append(c)
631	
632	        method_analysis = {}
633	        for method, conclusions in by_method.items():
634	            resolved = [c for c in conclusions if c.resolved is not None]
635	            if resolved:
636	                correct = sum(1 for c in resolved if c.resolved)
637	                accuracy = correct / len(resolved)
638	                avg_confidence = sum(c.confidence_expressed for c in conclusions) / len(conclusions)
639	            else:
640	                accuracy = None
641	                avg_confidence = sum(c.confidence_expressed for c in conclusions) / len(conclusions)
642	
643	            method_analysis[method] = {
644	                "count": len(conclusions),
645	                "resolved": len(resolved),
646	                "accuracy": accuracy,
647	                "average_confidence": avg_confidence,
648	            }
649	
650	        # Sort by accuracy
651	        sorted_methods = sorted(
652	            method_analysis.items(),
653	            key=lambda x: x[1]["accuracy"] if x[1]["accuracy"] is not None else -1,
654	            reverse=True
655	        )
656	
657	        return {
658	            "domain": domain,
659	            "total_conclusions": len(conclusions_in_domain),
660	            "resolved_conclusions": sum(1 for c in conclusions_in_domain if c.resolved is not None),
661	            "method_breakdown": dict(sorted_methods),
662	            "best_method": sorted_methods[0][0] if sorted_methods else None,
663	            "worst_method": sorted_methods[-1][0] if sorted_methods else None,
664	        }
665	
666	    def feedback_for_methodology(self) -> List[Dict]:
667	        """
668	        Generate specific feedback for the methodological brain.
669	
670	        This is the CRITICAL integration point. It transforms substantive
671	        accuracy data into METHODOLOGICAL observations that the main brain
672	        can store and learn from.
673	
674	        Produces observations like:
675	        - "Analogical reasoning has 0.35 accuracy in tech predictions.
676	           Consider supplementing with base-rate analysis."
677	        - "First-principles reasoning has 0.78 accuracy in competitive analysis.
678	           This is a methodological strength."
679	        - "The firm is systematically overconfident (avg 0.72, accuracy 0.55).
680	           Recommend calibration training."
681	
682	        These are formatted as METHODOLOGICAL claims about which methods work,
683	        not claims about the world.
684	
685	        Returns:
686	            List of feedback dictionaries with:
687	            - feedback_type: "strength" | "weakness" | "calibration" | "combination"
688	            - method_involved: str (method name or list of methods)
689	            - observation: str (the methodological claim)
690	            - domain: str (where applicable)
691	            - confidence: float (how confident in this feedback)
692	            - evidence: dict (supporting statistics)
693	            - recommendation: str (what to do about it)
694	        """
695	        feedback = []
696	        report = self.method_reliability_report()
697	
698	        if not report:
699	            return feedback
700	
701	        # 1. Method strength/weakness feedback
702	        for method, stats in report.items():
703	            if stats["resolved_conclusions"] < 3:
704	                # Not enough data
705	                continue
706	
707	            accuracy = stats["accuracy_rate"]
708	            if accuracy is None:
709	                continue
710	
711	            # Strength if accuracy > 0.65
712	            if accuracy > 0.65:
713	                feedback.append({
714	                    "feedback_type": "strength",
715	                    "method_involved": method,
716	                    "observation": (
717	                        f"{method.replace('_', ' ').title()} reasoning demonstrates "
718	                        f"strong reliability with {accuracy:.1%} accuracy "
719	                        f"({stats['resolved_conclusions']} predictions resolved)."
720	                    ),
721	                    "domain": "all",
722	                    "confidence": min(stats["resolved_conclusions"] / 10.0, 1.0),
723	                    "evidence": {
724	                        "accuracy_rate": accuracy,
725	                        "resolved_count": stats["resolved_conclusions"],
726	                        "total_count": stats["total_conclusions"],
727	                    },
728	                    "recommendation": (
729	                        f"Increase reliance on {method.replace('_', ' ')} reasoning. "
730	                        f"Consider using it as primary method where applicable."
731	                    ),
732	                })
733	
734	            # Weakness if accuracy < 0.45
735	            elif accuracy < 0.45:
736	                feedback.append({
737	                    "feedback_type": "weakness",
738	                    "method_involved": method,
739	                    "observation": (
740	                        f"{method.replace('_', ' ').title()} reasoning shows weak reliability "
741	                        f"with only {accuracy:.1%} accuracy "
742	                        f"({stats['resolved_conclusions']} predictions resolved)."
743	                    ),
744	                    "domain": "all",
745	                    "confidence": min(stats["resolved_conclusions"] / 10.0, 1.0),
746	                    "evidence": {
747	                        "accuracy_rate": accuracy,
748	                        "resolved_count": stats["resolved_conclusions"],
749	                        "total_count": stats["total_conclusions"],
750	                    },
751	                    "recommendation": (
752	                        f"Reduce reliance on {method.replace('_', ' ')} reasoning alone. "
753	                        f"Consider combining with other methods for validation."
754	                    ),
755	                })
756	
757	        # 2. Calibration feedback
758	        overconfident_methods = [
759	            (m, s) for m, s in report.items()
760	            if s["calibration_error"] and s["calibration_error"] > 0.15
761	            and s["average_confidence"] > (s["accuracy_rate"] or 0.0)
762	        ]
763	
764	        if overconfident_methods:
765	            feedback.append({
766	                "feedback_type": "calibration",
767	                "method_involved": [m for m, s in overconfident_methods],
768	                "observation": (
769	                    f"The firm shows systematic overconfidence across "
770	                    f"{len(overconfident_methods)} reasoning methods. "
771	                    f"Average confidence exceeds actual accuracy."
772	                ),
773	                "domain": "all",
774	                "confidence": 0.8,
775	                "evidence": {
776	                    "overconfident_methods": [
777	                        {
778	                            "method": m,
779	                            "avg_confidence": s["average_confidence"],
780	                            "accuracy": s["accuracy_rate"],
781	                            "gap": s["calibration_error"],
782	                        }
783	                        for m, s in overconfident_methods
784	                    ]
785	                },
786	                "recommendation": (
787	                    "Implement confidence calibration training. "
788	                    "When expressing confidence, systematically reduce by 10-15% "
789	                    "to account for overconfidence bias."
790	                ),
791	            })
792	
793	        # 3. Domain-specific strength/weakness
794	        all_conclusions = self.registry.conclusions.values()
795	        domains = set(c.domain for c in all_conclusions)
796	
797	        for domain in domains:
798	            domain_analysis = self.domain_report(domain)
799	            if "error" in domain_analysis:
800	                continue
801	
802	            best_method = domain_analysis.get("best_method")
803	            worst_method = domain_analysis.get("worst_method")
804	
805	            if best_method:
806	                method_stats = domain_analysis["method_breakdown"][best_method]
807	                if method_stats["accuracy"] and method_stats["accuracy"] > 0.60:
808	                    feedback.append({
809	                        "feedback_type": "domain_strength",
810	                        "method_involved": best_method,
811	                        "observation": (
812	                            f"{best_method.replace('_', ' ').title()} reasoning is particularly "
813	                            f"effective in {domain} domain with {method_stats['accuracy']:.1%} accuracy."
814	                        ),
815	                        "domain": domain,
816	                        "confidence": min(method_stats["resolved"] / 10.0, 1.0),
817	                        "evidence": {
818	                            "accuracy": method_stats["accuracy"],
819	                            "resolved_count": method_stats["resolved"],
820	                        },
821	                        "recommendation": (
822	                            f"Prioritize {best_method.replace('_', ' ')} reasoning "
823	                            f"for future {domain} analysis."
824	                        ),
825	                    })
826	
827	        # 4. Method combination feedback
828	        # Look for cases where combining methods might help
829	        if len(report) > 1:
830	            high_variance = [
831	                (m, s) for m, s in report.items()
832	                if s["resolved_conclusions"] > 2
833	                and 0.45 <= (s["accuracy_rate"] or 0.5) <= 0.65
834	            ]
835	
836	            if high_variance:
837	                feedback.append({
838	                    "feedback_type": "combination",
839	                    "method_involved": [m for m, s in high_variance],
840	                    "observation": (
841	                        f"Several methods show moderate performance "
842	                        f"({[m for m, s in high_variance]}). "
843	                        f"Combining complementary methods may improve reliability."
844	                    ),
845	                    "domain": "all",
846	                    "confidence": 0.6,
847	                    "evidence": {
848	                        "moderate_methods": [
849	                            {"method": m, "accuracy": s["accuracy_rate"]}
850	                            for m, s in high_variance
851	                        ]
852	                    },
853	                    "recommendation": (
854	                        f"When using {', '.join([m for m, s in high_variance])}, "
855	                        f"implement cross-validation with a second complementary method."
856	                    ),
857	                })
858	
859	        return feedback
860	
861	    def _generate_recommendations(
862	        self,
863	        method: str,
864	        overall: MethodAccuracyRecord,
865	        best_domains: List[str],
866	        worst_domains: List[str],
867	        is_calibrated: bool,
868	    ) -> List[str]:
869	        """Helper to generate specific recommendations for a method."""
870	        recommendations = []
871	
872	        if not overall.resolved_conclusions:
873	            recommendations.append(f"Gather more resolved predictions ({overall.resolved_conclusions} so far).")
874	            return recommendations
875	
876	        accuracy = overall.accuracy_rate or 0.0
877	
878	        if accuracy > 0.7:
879	            recommendations.append(f"Excellent track record. Increase reliance in all domains.")
880	            if best_domains:
881	                recommendations.append(f"Particularly strong in: {', '.join(best_domains)}")
882	        elif accuracy < 0.4:
883	            recommendations.append(f"Weak performance. Use only as secondary validation method.")
884	            if worst_domains:
885	                recommendations.append(f"Avoid using in: {', '.join(worst_domains)}")
886	        else:
887	            recommendations.append(f"Moderate performance. Use with caution, validate with other methods.")
888	
889	        if not is_calibrated:
890	            if overall.calibration_error and overall.calibration_error > 0.2:
891	                recommendations.append("Confidence is poorly calibrated. Recalibrate confidence thresholds.")
892	
893	        if best_domains and worst_domains:
894	            recommendations.append(
895	                f"Strong in {best_domains[0]}, weak in {worst_domains[0]}. "
896	                f"Investigate domain-specific factors."
897	            )
898	
899	        return recommendations
900

902	# ── AutoResolver ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
903	
904	class AutoResolver:
905	    """
906	    Semi-automatic resolution of predictions using Claude with web search.
907	
908	    Given a conclusion and the current date, attempts to determine:
909	    - Is this resolvable yet?
910	    - If yes, was it correct?
911	    - What evidence supports the resolution?
912	    """
913	
914	    def __init__(self, llm: LLMClient | None = None):
915	        """
916	        Initialize the resolver.
917	
918	        Args:
919	            llm: Optional LLM client (uses configured default if not provided)
920	        """
921	        self._llm = llm or llm_client_from_settings()
922	
923	    def check_resolvable(
924	        self,
925	        conclusion: SubstantiveConclusion
926	    ) -> Tuple[bool, Optional[bool], str]:
927	        """
928	        Check if a prediction is resolvable and, if so, determine its accuracy.
929	
930	        Uses Claude to reason about whether:
931	        1. The prediction can be checked yet (based on resolution_date)
932	        2. If resolvable, whether it turned out to be correct
933	        3. What evidence supports the determination
934	
935	        Args:
936	            conclusion: The conclusion to check
937	
938	        Returns:
939	            Tuple of (is_resolvable, was_correct, reasoning)
940	            - is_resolvable: True if we can check the prediction now
941	            - was_correct: True/False if resolvable, None if not
942	            - reasoning: Explanation of how it was determined
943	        """
944	        # Check if it's time to resolve
945	        if not conclusion.is_prediction:
946	            return False, None, "Not marked as a prediction"
947	
948	        if conclusion.resolved is not None:
949	            return False, None, "Already resolved"
950	
951	        if conclusion.resolution_date is None:
952	            return False, None, "No resolution date specified"
953	
954	        today = date.today()
955	        if today < conclusion.resolution_date:
956	            days_until = (conclusion.resolution_date - today).days
957	            return False, None, f"Too early to resolve (in {days_until} days)"
958	
959	        # Try to resolve using Claude
960	        try:
961	            prompt = f"""
962	Given this prediction made on {conclusion.episode_date}:
963	
964	"{conclusion.text}"
965	
966	Reasoning method: {conclusion.method_used}
967	Domain: {conclusion.domain}
968	Stated resolution condition: {conclusion.falsification_condition or "Not specified"}
969	Intended resolution date: {conclusion.resolution_date}
970	Today's date: {today}
971	
972	Determine:
973	1. Can this prediction be evaluated now? (Yes/No)
974	2. If yes, was it correct? (True/False/Partially True)
975	3. What evidence or information supports this determination?
976	
977	Format your response as:
978	RESOLVABLE: [Yes/No]
979	CORRECT: [True/False/Partially True/N/A]
980	EVIDENCE: [Brief explanation with sources where applicable]
981	"""
982	
983	            response_text = self._llm.complete(
984	                system="Follow the user's output format exactly.",
985	                user=prompt,
986	                max_tokens=500,
987	                temperature=0.0,
988	            )
989	
990	            # Parse response
991	            lines = response_text.strip().split('\n')
992	            resolvable = False
993	            correct = None
994	            evidence = ""
995	
996	            for line in lines:
997	                if line.startswith("RESOLVABLE:"):
998	                    resolvable = "yes" in line.lower()
999	                elif line.startswith("CORRECT:"):
1000	                    if "true" in line.lower() and "partially" not in line.lower():
1001	                        correct = True
1002	                    elif "false" in line.lower():
1003	                        correct = False
1004	                    # Partially True or N/A → return None
1005	                elif line.startswith("EVIDENCE:"):
1006	                    evidence = line.replace("EVIDENCE:", "").strip()
1007	
1008	            if not evidence:
1009	                evidence = response_text
1010	
1011	            return resolvable, correct, evidence
1012	
1013	        except Exception as e:
1014	            logger.error(f"Error checking resolvable conclusion {conclusion.id}: {e}")
1015	            return False, None, f"Error during resolution check: {str(e)}"
1016

294	class Claim(BaseModel):

296	    An atomic proposition extracted from a transcript segment.

300	    speaker, and positioned in embedding space.

307	    segment_context: str = ""                   # Surrounding paragraph for disambiguation
308	    disciplines: list[Discipline] = []          # Relevant domains
309	    embedding: Optional[list[float]] = None     # SBERT embedding vector
310	    confidence: float = 1.0                     # Extraction confidence (0-1)
311	    timestamp_seconds: Optional[float] = None   # Position in episode
312	    source_id: str = ""                         # Source artifact or chunk ID
313	    voice_id: str = ""                          # Voice profile ID
314	    # Round 3 additions

316	    last_validated_at: Optional[datetime] = None

319	class Principle(BaseModel):
320	    """
321	    A distilled, stable belief held by the firm.
322	
323	    Principles are derived from clusters of claims across multiple episodes.
324	    They represent the firm's enduring intellectual commitments — the things
325	    the founders believe deeply enough to act on repeatedly.
326	    """
327	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
328	    text: str                                   # Canonical statement
329	    description: str = ""                       # Elaborated explanation
330	    disciplines: list[Discipline] = []
331	    conviction: ConvictionLevel = ConvictionLevel.MODERATE
332	    conviction_score: float = 0.5               # Continuous 0-1
333	    embedding: Optional[list[float]] = None
334	
335	    # Evidence trail
336	    supporting_claims: list[str] = []           # Claim IDs
337	    first_appeared: Optional[date] = None
338	    last_reinforced: Optional[date] = None
339	    mention_count: int = 0
340	
341	    # Coherence metrics (from the 6-layer engine)

9	def synthetic_claim(text: str, *, slot: str) -> Claim:
10	    """Minimal Claim for API-only coherence (no firm linkage)."""
11	    return Claim(
12	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
13	        text=text.strip(),

1	"""Calibration metrics on researcher-supplied rows (no Store / no tenant data)."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from noosphere.scoring import brier_score, log_loss_binary
6	
7	
8	def prob_mid_row(prob_low: float, prob_high: float) -> float:
9	    return 0.5 * (float(prob_low) + float(prob_high))
10	
11	
12	def score_prediction_rows(rows: list[tuple[float, int]]) -> dict:
13	    """Each row: (probability midpoint, outcome 0|1)."""
14	    if not rows:
15	        return {
16	            "n": 0,
17	            "mean_brier": None,
18	            "mean_log_loss": None,
19	            "calibration_bins": [],
20	        }
21	    bs = [brier_score(p, y) for p, y in rows]
22	    ls = [log_loss_binary(p, y) for p, y in rows]
23	    hits = [0] * 10
24	    totals = [0] * 10
25	    for p, y in rows:
26	        idx = min(9, max(0, int(p * 10)))
27	        totals[idx] += 1
28	        hits[idx] += y
29	    bins: list[dict] = []
30	    for i in range(10):
31	        lo, hi = i / 10, (i + 1) / 10
32	        n = totals[i]
33	        rate = (hits[i] + 0.5) / (n + 1.0) if n else None
34	        bins.append({"bin_low": lo, "bin_high": hi, "n": n, "empirical_rate": rate})
35	    return {
36	        "n": len(rows),
37	        "mean_brier": sum(bs) / len(bs),
38	        "mean_log_loss": sum(ls) / len(ls),
39	        "calibration_bins": bins,
40	    }
41

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from typing import Callable
4	
5	from noosphere.models import CascadeEdgeRelation, Method
6	
7	
8	class MethodCollisionError(Exception):
9	    pass
10	
11	
12	class MethodNotFoundError(Exception):
13	    pass
14	
15	
16	def _parse_semver(version: str) -> tuple[int, ...]:
17	    parts: list[int] = []
18	    for p in version.split("."):
19	        try:
20	            parts.append(int(p))
21	        except ValueError:
22	            parts.append(0)
23	    return tuple(parts)
24	
25	
26	class MethodRegistry:
27	    def __init__(self) -> None:
28	        self._specs: dict[tuple[str, str], Method] = {}
29	        self._fns: dict[tuple[str, str], Callable] = {}
30	        self._emits_edges: dict[str, list[CascadeEdgeRelation]] = {}
31	
32	    def register(self, spec: Method, fn: Callable) -> None:
33	        key = (spec.name, spec.version)
34	        if key in self._specs:
35	            raise MethodCollisionError(
36	                f"Method {spec.name} v{spec.version} already registered"
37	            )
38	        self._specs[key] = spec
39	        self._fns[key] = fn
40	
41	    def set_emits_edges(
42	        self, method_id: str, edges: list[CascadeEdgeRelation]
43	    ) -> None:
44	        self._emits_edges[method_id] = list(edges)
45	
46	    def get_emits_edges(self, method_id: str) -> list[CascadeEdgeRelation]:
47	        return self._emits_edges.get(method_id, [])
48	
49	    def get(
50	        self,
51	        name: str,
52	        version: str = "latest",
53	        include_retired: bool = False,
54	    ) -> tuple[Method, Callable]:
55	        if version != "latest":
56	            key = (name, version)
57	            if key not in self._specs:
58	                raise MethodNotFoundError(
59	                    f"Method {name} v{version} not found"
60	                )
61	            spec = self._specs[key]
62	            if spec.status == "retired" and not include_retired:
63	                raise MethodNotFoundError(
64	                    f"Method {name} v{version} is retired"
65	                )
66	            return spec, self._fns[key]
67	
68	        candidates: list[tuple[str, Method]] = []
69	        for (n, v), spec in self._specs.items():
70	            if n != name:
71	                continue
72	            if spec.status == "retired" and not include_retired:
73	                continue
74	            candidates.append((v, spec))
75	
76	        if not candidates:
77	            raise MethodNotFoundError(f"No active method found for {name}")
78	
79	        candidates.sort(key=lambda x: _parse_semver(x[0]), reverse=True)
80	        best_version = candidates[0][0]
81	        return self._specs[(name, best_version)], self._fns[(name, best_version)]
82	
83	    def list(self, status_filter: str | None = None) -> list[Method]:
84	        specs = list(self._specs.values())
85	        if status_filter is not None:
86	            specs = [s for s in specs if s.status == status_filter]
87	        return specs
88	
89	
90	REGISTRY = MethodRegistry()
91

23	from noosphere.methods._hooks import _FAILURE_HOOKS, _POST_HOOKS, _PRE_HOOKS
24	from noosphere.methods._registry import REGISTRY

38	def set_store_factory(factory: Callable[..., Any]) -> None:

45	        try:
46	            return _store_factory()

52	def _canonical_json(obj: Any) -> str:
53	    if isinstance(obj, BaseModel):
54	        obj = obj.model_dump(mode="json")
55	    return json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), default=str)

58	def _current_git_sha() -> str:
59	    sha = os.environ.get("THESEUS_GIT_SHA")
60	    if sha:
61	        return sha
62	    try:
63	        result = subprocess.run(
64	            ["git", "rev-parse", "HEAD"],
65	            capture_output=True,
66	            text=True,
67	            timeout=5,
68	        )
69	        if result.returncode == 0:
70	            return result.stdout.strip()
71	    except Exception:
72	        pass
73	    return "unknown"
74	
75	
76	def _current_correlation_id() -> str:
77	    return CORRELATION_ID.get()
78	
79	
80	def _current_tenant_id() -> str:
81	    return TENANT_ID.get()
82	
83	
84	def _validate_against_schema(schema: Any, data: Any) -> Any:
85	    if isinstance(schema, type) and issubclass(schema, BaseModel):
86	        if isinstance(data, schema):
87	            return data
88	        return schema.model_validate(data)
89	    if isinstance(schema, dict) and schema:
90	        try:
91	            import jsonschema as _js
92	
93	            _js.validate(data, schema)
94	        except ImportError:
95	            pass
96	    return data
97	
98	
99	def _utcnow() -> datetime:
100	    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
101	
102	
103	def _run_failure_path(
104	    spec: Method,
105	    inv: MethodInvocation,
106	    validated_input: Any,
107	    exc: BaseException,
108	) -> None:
109	    inv = inv.model_copy(
110	        update={
111	            "ended_at": _utcnow(),
112	            "succeeded": False,
113	            "error_kind": type(exc).__name__,
114	        }
115	    )
116	    for hook_name, hook in _FAILURE_HOOKS:
117	        try:
118	            hook(spec, inv, validated_input, exc)
119	        except Exception:
120	            logger.exception("Failure hook %s raised", hook_name)
121	    store = _get_store()
122	    if store is not None:
123	        try:
124	            store.insert_method_invocation(inv)
125	        except Exception:
126	            logger.exception("Failed to persist invocation")
127	
128	
129	def register_method(
130	    *,
131	    name: str,
132	    version: str,
133	    method_type: MethodType,
134	    input_schema: Any,
135	    output_schema: Any,
136	    description: str,
137	    rationale: str,
138	    preconditions: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
139	    postconditions: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
140	    dependencies: list[tuple[str, str]] | tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (),
141	    owner: str,
142	    status: str = "active",
143	    nondeterministic: bool = False,
144	    emits_edges: list[CascadeEdgeRelation] | tuple[CascadeEdgeRelation, ...] = (),
145	) -> Callable:
146	    def decorator(fn: Callable) -> Callable:
147	        if isinstance(input_schema, type) and issubclass(input_schema, BaseModel):
148	            in_schema_dict = input_schema.model_json_schema()
149	        elif isinstance(input_schema, dict):
150	            in_schema_dict = input_schema

150	            in_schema_dict = input_schema
151	        else:
152	            in_schema_dict = {}
153	
154	        if isinstance(output_schema, type) and issubclass(output_schema, BaseModel):
155	            out_schema_dict = output_schema.model_json_schema()
156	        elif isinstance(output_schema, dict):
157	            out_schema_dict = output_schema
158	        else:
159	            out_schema_dict = {}
160	
161	        impl = MethodImplRef(
162	            module=fn.__module__,
163	            fn_name=fn.__qualname__,
164	            git_sha=_current_git_sha(),
165	            image_digest=None,
166	        )
167	
168	        spec_for_hash = {
169	            "name": name,
170	            "version": version,
171	            "method_type": method_type.value,
172	            "input_schema": in_schema_dict,
173	            "output_schema": out_schema_dict,
174	            "description": description,
175	            "rationale": rationale,
176	            "preconditions": list(preconditions),
177	            "postconditions": list(postconditions),
178	            "dependencies": [list(d) for d in dependencies],
179	            "implementation": impl.model_dump(mode="json"),
180	            "owner": owner,
181	            "status": status,
182	            "nondeterministic": nondeterministic,
183	        }
184	
185	        method_id = hashlib.sha256(
186	            _canonical_json(spec_for_hash).encode()
187	        ).hexdigest()[:32]
188	
189	        spec = Method(
190	            method_id=method_id,
191	            name=name,
192	            version=version,
193	            method_type=method_type,
194	            input_schema=in_schema_dict,
195	            output_schema=out_schema_dict,
196	            description=description,
197	            rationale=rationale,
198	            preconditions=list(preconditions),
199	            postconditions=list(postconditions),
200	            dependencies=list(dependencies),
201	            implementation=impl,
202	            owner=owner,
203	            status=status,
204	            nondeterministic=nondeterministic,
205	            created_at=_utcnow(),
206	        )
207	
208	        @wraps(fn)
209	        def wrapped(input_data: Any) -> Any:
210	            validated_input = _validate_against_schema(input_schema, input_data)
211	            input_hash = hashlib.sha256(
212	                _canonical_json(validated_input).encode()
213	            ).hexdigest()
214	
215	            inv = MethodInvocation(
216	                id=str(uuid4()),
217	                method_id=spec.method_id,
218	                input_hash=input_hash,
219	                output_hash="",
220	                started_at=_utcnow(),
221	                ended_at=None,
222	                succeeded=False,
223	                error_kind=None,
224	                correlation_id=_current_correlation_id(),
225	                tenant_id=_current_tenant_id(),
226	            )
227	
228	            try:
229	                for _hook_name, hook in _PRE_HOOKS:
230	                    hook(spec, inv, validated_input)
231	            except Exception as e:
232	                _run_failure_path(spec, inv, validated_input, e)
233	                raise
234	
235	            try:
236	                result = fn(validated_input)
237	                _validate_against_schema(output_schema, result)
238	                output_hash = hashlib.sha256(
239	                    _canonical_json(result).encode()
240	                ).hexdigest()
241	                inv = inv.model_copy(
242	                    update={
243	                        "ended_at": _utcnow(),
244	                        "succeeded": True,
245	                        "output_hash": output_hash,
246	                    }
247	                )
248	
249	                for hook_name, hook in _POST_HOOKS:
250	                    try:
251	                        hook(spec, inv, validated_input, result)
252	                    except Exception:
253	                        logger.exception("Post-hook %s raised", hook_name)
254	
255	                store = _get_store()
256	                if store is not None:
257	                    try:
258	                        store.insert_method_invocation(inv)
259	                    except Exception:
260	                        logger.exception("Failed to persist invocation")
261	
262	                return result
263	            except Exception as e:
264	                _run_failure_path(spec, inv, validated_input, e)
265	                raise
266	
267	        wrapped.__method_spec__ = spec  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
268	
269	        REGISTRY.register(spec, wrapped)
270	
271	        if emits_edges:
272	            REGISTRY.set_emits_edges(method_id, list(emits_edges))
273	
274	        store = _get_store()
275	        if store is not None:
276	            try:
277	                store.insert_method(spec)
278	            except Exception:
279	                logger.exception("Failed to persist method spec")
280	
281	        return wrapped
282	
283	    return decorator
284

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from typing import Any, Callable
4	
5	from noosphere.models import Method, MethodInvocation
6	
7	PreHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any], None]
8	PostHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any, Any], None]
9	FailureHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any, BaseException], None]
10	
11	_PRE_HOOKS: list[tuple[str, PreHook]] = []
12	_POST_HOOKS: list[tuple[str, PostHook]] = []
13	_FAILURE_HOOKS: list[tuple[str, FailureHook]] = []
14	
15	
16	def _replace_or_append(
17	    lst: list[tuple[str, Callable]], name: str, hook: Callable
18	) -> None:
19	    for i, (n, _) in enumerate(lst):
20	        if n == name:
21	            lst[i] = (name, hook)
22	            return
23	    lst.append((name, hook))
24	
25	
26	def register_pre_hook(name: str, hook: PreHook) -> None:
27	    _replace_or_append(_PRE_HOOKS, name, hook)
28	
29	
30	def register_post_hook(name: str, hook: PostHook) -> None:
31	    _replace_or_append(_POST_HOOKS, name, hook)
32	
33	
34	def register_failure_hook(name: str, hook: FailureHook) -> None:
35	    _replace_or_append(_FAILURE_HOOKS, name, hook)
36	
37	
38	def unregister_hook(name: str) -> None:
39	    for lst in (_PRE_HOOKS, _POST_HOOKS, _FAILURE_HOOKS):
40	        lst[:] = [(n, h) for n, h in lst if n != name]
41

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import logging
4	from collections import deque
5	from datetime import datetime, timezone
6	from typing import Optional
7	from uuid import uuid4
8	
9	from noosphere.models import (
10	    CascadeEdge,
11	    CascadeEdgeRelation,
12	    CascadeNode,
13	    CascadeNodeKind,
14	)

18	
19	class CascadeCycleError(Exception):
20	    """Adding this edge would create a cycle in the depends_on subgraph."""
21	
22	
23	class CascadeGraph:
24	    def __init__(self, store) -> None:  # noqa: ANN001
25	        self._store = store
26	
27	    # ── node helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28	
29	    def add_node(
30	        self,
31	        *,
32	        kind: CascadeNodeKind,
33	        ref: str,
34	        attrs: Optional[dict] = None,
35	        node_id: Optional[str] = None,
36	    ) -> str:
37	        nid = node_id or str(uuid4())
38	        node = CascadeNode(
39	            node_id=nid,
40	            kind=kind,
41	            ref=ref,
42	            attrs=attrs or {},
43	        )
44	        self._store.insert_cascade_node(node)
45	        return nid
46	
47	    # ── edge helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
48	
49	    def add_edge(
50	        self,
51	        *,
52	        src: str,
53	        dst: str,
54	        relation: CascadeEdgeRelation,
55	        method_invocation_id: str,
56	        confidence: float,
57	        unresolved: bool = False,
58	    ) -> str:
59	        from noosphere.store import CascadeEdgeConflictError, CascadeEdgeOrphanError
60	
61	        inv = self._store.get_method_invocation(method_invocation_id)
62	        if inv is None:
63	            raise CascadeEdgeOrphanError(
64	                f"method_invocation_id {method_invocation_id!r} not found"
65	            )
66	
67	        if relation in (CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS, CascadeEdgeRelation.REFUTES):
68	            opposite = (
69	                CascadeEdgeRelation.REFUTES
70	                if relation == CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS
71	                else CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS
72	            )
73	            for existing in self._store.iter_cascade_edges(
74	                src=src, dst=dst, relation=opposite.value, include_retracted=False
75	            ):
76	                raise CascadeEdgeConflictError(
77	                    f"Non-retracted {opposite.value} edge exists "
78	                    f"between {src} -> {dst}"
79	                )
80	
81	        if relation == CascadeEdgeRelation.DEPENDS_ON:
82	            self._check_cycle(src, dst)
83	
84	        edge_id = str(uuid4())
85	        edge = CascadeEdge(
86	            edge_id=edge_id,
87	            src=src,
88	            dst=dst,
89	            relation=relation,
90	            method_invocation_id=method_invocation_id,
91	            confidence=confidence,
92	            unresolved=unresolved,
93	            established_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
94	        )
95	        self._store.insert_cascade_edge(edge)
96	        return edge_id
97	
98	    def retract_edge(self, edge_id: str) -> None:
99	        self._store.retract_cascade_edge(edge_id, datetime.now(timezone.utc))
100	
101	    def iter_edges(
102	        self,
103	        *,
104	        src: Optional[str] = None,
105	        dst: Optional[str] = None,
106	        relation: Optional[str] = None,
107	        include_retracted: bool = False,
108	    ):
109	        yield from self._store.iter_cascade_edges(
110	            src=src, dst=dst, relation=relation, include_retracted=include_retracted
111	        )
112	
113	    # ── cycle detection ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
114	
115	    def _check_cycle(self, src: str, dst: str) -> None:
116	        """BFS forward from dst in depends_on subgraph; if we reach src it's a cycle."""
117	        visited: set[str] = set()
118	        queue: deque[str] = deque([dst])
119	        while queue:
120	            current = queue.popleft()
121	            if current == src:
122	                raise CascadeCycleError(
123	                    f"Adding depends_on edge {src} -> {dst} would create a cycle"
124	                )
125	            if current in visited:
126	                continue
127	            visited.add(current)
128	            for edge in self._store.iter_cascade_edges(
129	                src=current,
130	                relation=CascadeEdgeRelation.DEPENDS_ON.value,
131	                include_retracted=False,
132	            ):
133	                queue.append(edge.dst)
134

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from collections import deque
4	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
5	from typing import Optional
6	
7	from noosphere.models import CascadeEdge, CascadeEdgeRelation
8	
9	
10	@dataclass
11	class CutReport:
12	    """Result of cutting a node from the cascade graph."""
13	    cut_node_id: str
14	    affected_edges: list[CascadeEdge] = field(default_factory=list)
15	    orphaned_nodes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
16	    confidence_deltas: dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=dict)
17	
18	
19	def explain(store, node_id: str) -> list[CascadeEdge]:
20	    """Return all non-retracted edges pointing INTO node_id (its evidence basis)."""
21	    return list(store.iter_cascade_edges(dst=node_id, include_retracted=False))
22	
23	
24	def downstream(store, node_id: str) -> list[CascadeEdge]:
25	    """Return all non-retracted edges whose src is node_id (what it supports)."""
26	    return list(store.iter_cascade_edges(src=node_id, include_retracted=False))
27	
28	
29	def cut(store, node_id: str) -> CutReport:
30	    """Simulate cutting a node: retract its outgoing edges and compute confidence deltas."""
31	    report = CutReport(cut_node_id=node_id)
32	
33	    outgoing = list(store.iter_cascade_edges(src=node_id, include_retracted=False))
34	    report.affected_edges = outgoing
35	
36	    affected_dst_nodes: set[str] = set()
37	    for edge in outgoing:
38	        affected_dst_nodes.add(edge.dst)
39	
40	    for dst_node in affected_dst_nodes:
41	        incoming = list(store.iter_cascade_edges(dst=dst_node, include_retracted=False))
42	        support_edges = [
43	            e for e in incoming
44	            if e.relation in (
45	                CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS,
46	                CascadeEdgeRelation.EXTRACTED_FROM,
47	                CascadeEdgeRelation.AGGREGATES,
48	                CascadeEdgeRelation.DEPENDS_ON,
49	            )
50	        ]
51	        total_confidence = sum(e.confidence for e in support_edges)
52	        cut_confidence = sum(
53	            e.confidence for e in support_edges if e.src == node_id
54	        )
55	        delta = -cut_confidence if total_confidence > 0 else 0.0
56	        report.confidence_deltas[dst_node] = delta
57	
58	        remaining = [e for e in support_edges if e.src != node_id]
59	        if not remaining:
60	            report.orphaned_nodes.append(dst_node)
61	
62	    return report
63
